description: Event planning, organization, budgeting, logistics, party preparation, scheduling, vendor management, guest coordination, and practical mathematics for birthday celebrations
research_alignment: Executive function, planning skills, practical mathematics, organizational behavior, budgeting and financial planning, logistics management, communication skills, time management, decision-making
research_sources: Event Planning Mathematics: Cost calculations, catering per-person pricing, rental logistics
Executive Function: Planning skills, organization, decision-making under constraints
Practical Mathematics: Budgeting, fractions, percentages, measurements, venue capacity calculations
Event Management Best Practices: Timeline development, contingency planning, vendor coordination

kids_4_under.clue: You need this many people to have a birthday party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is more than one?

kids_4_under.clue: This is what you do when you plan a party - you think ahead
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is planning?

kids_4_under.clue: This is the day your party happens
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is your birthday?

kids_4_under.clue: You make a list of all the people who will come to your party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a guest list?

kids_4_under.clue: This money is what you spend on your birthday party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a budget?

kids_4_under.clue: You send this to people to invite them to your party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is an invitation?

kids_4_under.clue: This is where your birthday party happens
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a location or venue?

kids_4_under.clue: These are the things you need to do to get ready for the party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What are tasks or chores?

kids_4_under.clue: You need this much time to plan a really good party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is advance time or a few weeks?

kids_4_under.clue: This is the plan for what happens during your party from start to finish
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a schedule or agenda?

kids_4_under.clue: You count these to see how much money you have to spend
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What are dollars or coins?

kids_4_under.clue: This person helps deliver the birthday cake you ordered
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a baker or delivery person?

kids_4_under.clue: You write this down to remember all the things you need to buy
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a shopping list or checklist?

kids_4_under.clue: This is the day before your party when you check if everything is ready
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is the day before or preparation day?

kids_4_under.clue: You do this to tell your friends when and where the party is
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is send an invitation or tell them?

kids_4_under.clue: This tells you what day the party is and what time it starts
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is the date and time?

kids_4_under.clue: You count these to know how much food to buy
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What are the number of guests?

kids_4_under.clue: This is something you decide on before you plan anything - like a superhero theme
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a theme?

kids_4_under.clue: You buy more than you think you need in case people eat extra
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is extra food?

kids_4_under.clue: This is when things go wrong and you have a backup plan
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a problem or emergency?

kids_4_under.clue: You do this at the party to make sure all the guests have fun
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is coordinate activities or manage the party?

kids_4_under.clue: This is a list of people who said they will come to your party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What are RSVP confirmations or yes responses?

kids_4_under.clue: You use these to hold decorations like balloons and banners at the party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is tape, string, or clips?

kids_4_under.clue: This is the person who brings the food or decorations to your party venue
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a vendor or service provider?

kids_4_under.clue: You need to know this about each guest so you can plan the right amount of food
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is if they have food allergies?

kids_4_under.clue: This person helps you set up all the decorations before the party starts
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a helper or volunteer?

kids_4_under.clue: You do this the night before to prepare games, decorations, and food
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is set up or get ready?

kids_4_under.clue: This is the time when everyone will eat at your party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is lunch time, dinner time, or snack time?

kids_4_under.clue: You make this so you know exactly how much each person will spend on the party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a per-person cost or budget breakdown?

kids_4_under.clue: This is what you do if not many people said they would come to your party
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is send more invitations or reschedule?

kids_4_under.clue: You check this to see if your party supplies arrived on time
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is the delivery date or tracking?

kids_4_under.clue: This is a list of activities planned for your party in order
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is an activity schedule or agenda?

kids_4_under.clue: You do this with friends and family to help prepare for the big day
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is ask for help or delegate tasks?

kids_4_under.clue: This is what you need to know about the party place before booking it
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is the size, location, and amenities?

kids_4_under.clue: You keep track of this to make sure you are not spending too much money

kids_5_9.clue: This is the type of food you'll serve - pizza, hot dogs, or something else
kids_5_9.correctQuestion: What is a catering option or menu?

kids_5_9.clue: You should add this percentage to your budget as a safety buffer
kids_5_9.correctQuestion: What is 10-15 percent?

kids_5_9.clue: This is how you divided tasks fairly among people helping with the party

kids_5_9.clue: You need to find this for your party - it tells you parking, room size, and amenities

kids_5_9.correctQuestion: What are expense categories?

kids_5_9.correctQuestion: What is a contract or booking confirmation?

kids_5_9.correctQuestion: What is a recipe ratio or serving measurement?

kids_5_9.clue: If 40 percent of guests want vegetarian food, how many of 60 guests is that?
kids_5_9.correctQuestion: What is 24 guests?

kids_5_9.clue: You do this to all the items on your checklist as you complete them

kids_5_9.correctQuestion: What is a guest database or spreadsheet?

kids_5_9.correctQuestion: What is 60 dollars?

kids_10_13.correctQuestion: What is 25 percent?

kids_10_13.correctQuestion: What is 7 packs?

kids_10_13.correctQuestion: What is 540 dollars?

kids_10_13.correctQuestion: What is 7 tables? (rounding up from 6.25)

high_school.correctQuestion: What is iterative planning or sprint-based organizing?

high_school.clue: Your total party budget is 2,000 dollars. If 35 percent goes to catering, how much is left for other expenses?
high_school.correctQuestion: What is 1,300 dollars?

high_school.correctQuestion: What is 18 dollars per person?

high_school.correctQuestion: What is 4 dollars per person?

high_school.correctQuestion: What is 100 dollars over budget?

high_school.correctQuestion: What is 5 cases for a cost of 175 dollars?

high_school.correctQuestion: What is a requirements analysis or status assessment?

high_school.correctQuestion: What is a statement of work (SOW) or contract addendum?

high_school.correctQuestion: What is KPI (Key Performance Indicator) tracking?

high_school.correctQuestion: What is contingency ratio or buffer percentage?

high_school.correctQuestion: What is the critical path method (CPM) or PERT analysis?

high_school.correctQuestion: What is 25 guests?

adults.clue: This behavioral economics principle explains why planning parties early reduces cognitive load and decision fatigue
adults.correctQuestion: What is the concept of decision fatigue or ego depletion?

adults.correctQuestion: What is working memory or cognitive load management?

adults.correctQuestion: What is the Eisenhower Matrix or priority quadrant?

adults.correctQuestion: What is predictive analytics or Bayesian estimation?

adults.correctQuestion: What is opportunity cost analysis or constraint satisfaction?

adults.correctQuestion: What is Agile or Scrum methodology?

adults.clue: Calculate the confidence interval for 65 confirmed guests when historical no-show rate is 12 percent
adults.correctQuestion: What is 57-59 guests (with 95% confidence)?

adults.clue: Your budget is 5,000 dollars, allocate to venue (40%), catering (45%), and contingency (15%)
adults.correctQuestion: What is 2,000 venue, 2,250 catering, 750 contingency?

adults.clue: Calculate break-even guest count if fixed venue cost is 800 and variable per-person cost is 25
adults.correctQuestion: What is approximately 32 guests (800 ÷ 25)?

adults.clue: Three vendors quote: A at 1,500, B at 1,350 (10% discount for 30-day advance), C at 1,600. Which is optimal?
Calculate total event cost: 100 guests at 35 per person catering, plus 500 venue, plus 200 decorations
adults.correctQuestion: What is 4,200 dollars?
